context: Planned deploy of capability-y v2 to general availability following successful eval and review.
decision: Deploy capability-y v2 to general availability with standard monitoring.
rationale: Safety eval passed with no open regressions; staged rollout was considered unnecessary given the low-risk change surface.

context: External report plus internal monitoring show that capability-y v2 is producing responses in a restricted advice domain that v1 correctly declined. Reproducible; affects roughly 0.3 percent of matched queries.
decision: Classify as a P1 safety incident; escalate to the on-call reviewer and begin the rollback decision process.
rationale: Reproducibility, category of affected queries, and volume cross the P1 threshold in the incident policy.

context: Emergency decision under the P1 incident classified in incident-2026-04-14-report. Options evaluated under a one-hour time budget.
decision: Roll back capability-y to v1 globally; disable v2 behind the feature flag.
options: Narrow v2 audience to internal only
Keep v2 and add a guard rule
rationale: Rollback was the only option that fully removes the observed regression within the incident time budget. A narrower audience still exposes enterprise users; a guard rule was not feasible to implement and test in the available window.

context: Post-incident review completed. The regression from incident-2026-04-14-report has been reproduced in the eval harness, a targeted fix has been implemented and tested, and a follow-up eval has been signed off.
decision: Redeploy capability-y v2.1 (patched) to general availability; close the feature flag on v2; retain the rollback as the fallback posture until v2.1 has 72 hours of clean monitoring.
options: Replace capability-y with an alternative design
rationale: The fix is narrowly scoped, covered by a new regression test, and verified against the follow-up eval. Retaining the rollback indefinitely is not justified given the verified fix.
